name: spider-lair
summary: A cave choked with webbing — sticky floor-sheets, silk-wrapped victims hanging from above, egg sacs pulsing gently in dark corners.

primary_palette.note: a flattened web-dais where the big one rests
trap funnel at an entrance — one-way snare

story_prompts: a cocoon still struggles — saved now or watched forever
the matriarch is gone raiding; the nursery is guarded by dozens of young
a drow handler lives alongside the brood, training them
something larger than the spiders ate the matriarch recently
anti_patterns: don't web the entire floor — leave clear stone lanes for the spiders' own use
cocoons hang from walls and ceiling, not standing on the floor
egg sacs cluster together — a nursery, not scattered
